name: Wesley Bell
subject: Just by the skin of our teeth — thank you!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
Peter, I wanted to take a moment to personally thank you for your dedication and hard work last month.

Because of this grassroots movement, my team and I saw a significant uptick in donations and we hit our end-of-month fundraising goal for April — _but it was close._

I know that during off years like this — especially as we get closer to spring and summer — it can feel exhausting to open your inbox to yet another campaign email asking you to chip in. And as the year goes on, with growing uncertainty under this extreme and unpredictable administration, it can be tough to keep momentum and morale high.

**But I'm here to remind you that things are not beyond repair. This campaign, our movement, and others like it remain committed to fighting for the people we promised to serve. There are still good leaders and good people refusing to give up — and it’s only possible because of supporters like you standing with us.**

Thank you for continuing to show up for Team Bell — your support means everything.

Onward,

name: Karen Bass
subject: The progress we’ve made
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Re-Elect Karen Bass for Mayor 2026 (FPPC ID #1471359)
body:
Friend,

We recently marked 100 days since our community faced a historic and unimaginable tragedy in the Palisades fire and other wildfires throughout the region.

The Army Corps of Engineers has worked hard to not just to help us rebuild on schedule, but ahead of schedule, removing debris, repairing damaged buildings, and rebuilding what we lost. This week we announced crews have now successfully cleared debris from more than 1,000 homes in the Palisades, marking a significant milestone in the community's recovery from the devastating January fires.

My top priority has always been to get people back home and back to their lives — building houses and rebuilding the community places that we hold dear, like our libraries, schools, and recreation centers.

We’ve cleared hazardous materials at a record pace. We restored water and power to standing homes in less than two months. And I used every tool I could to cut through the red tape, help to relocate impacted schools and childcare facilities, provide tax relief to small businesses impacted by the fire, and have expedited the process to rebuild homes that are protected from future wildfires.

While our lives and our community will never be the same, by working tirelessly and working together, we can build back better. For more information about the return and rebuild process, please visit recovery.lacity.gov [http://recovery.lacity.gov] .

Thanks for all you do,

Karen Bass Signature [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP24/1/94612/images/Karen%20Bass%20Signature%20Vector%202.png]

name: Sydney Kamlager
subject: They Have No Stones
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SYDNEY KAMLAGER-DOVE FOR CONGRESS]( "PAID FOR BY SYDNEY KAMLAGER-DOVE FOR CONGRESS")
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/47e4be8125de/fighting-for-this-country-14055067?e=e6ba7d1bc4)
---
| |
---
|
---
|
---
| Dear Peter:
We hit a new low in Judiciary.

This week in the Judiciary Committee, we voted on their immigration markup bill. The bill contains billions of dollars for ICE, a department that seems to need new cars, computers and $45,000 bonuses for their staff.

Bills represent the Administration’s values. The point of committee is to debate or defend policies. You would think Republicans would want to be on record defending their President, and yet the wildest thing happened. Republicans were silent. They sat in their seats, staring at the ceiling or rubbing their bald heads (that was Chip Roy), and not saying a damn word.

**There was a lot to say. Why?**
* Republicans voted to detain, deport, seize, and kidnap Americans.
* Republicans voted to allow January 6 insurrectionists to be hired by the federal government, including the Department of Homeland Security.
* Republicans voted to allow ICE to strip search children and lock them up in Guantanamo Bay.
* Republicans voted to allow ICE to enter domestic violence shelters.
* Republicans voted to prevent civil charges from being filed against the President.
* Republicans voted to arrest judges.

!?️!?️!?️!?️!?️!?️!?️!?️!?️!?️!?️!?️!?️!?️

They didn't speak up, [but I certainly did](https://sydneykamlager.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7fbfb0a1bdf4e44789fab92d0&id=3cdef79b23&e=e6ba7d1bc4).
---
| [ ](https://sydneykamlager.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7fbfb0a1bdf4e44789fab92d0&id=f78c398821&e=e6ba7d1bc4)
---
The DOGe had the Republicans' tongues in this hearing.
|
These Republicans are 🤬🤬 cowards. They refused to debate or defend the bills because they knew all of this is wrong and illegal. They don’t have the stones to stand up for this country, so all they can do is scowl at us from across the aisle. Shame.

As Dr. King said, “there comes a time when silence is betrayal.”

I am not staying silent.

**I am showing up, standing up, and speaking up.**

The markup bill passed out of committee because we are in the Minority, but I made sure that Republicans left shamed, politically bloodied and deeply vulnerable.

These policies they voted for, coupled with their tanking of the economy, their proposed cuts to Medicaid, Medicare and Social Security, and their terrorizing of America, are deeply unpopular with the American people.

The bad news is that this will be a long, protracted battle and we are not even through the 1st quarter.

The real news is that we all have a role to play and winning this fight will take all of us working together.

The good news is that people are starting to see these maniacs for who they are. We can’t let up.

**We must remind them all the way through 2026.**

---
|
---

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5e82612b-7ffd-4d36-940f-9a3bf6d53b2f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: FROM SPEAKER MIKE JOHNSON
subject: I'm sounding the alarm
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Here’s the situation

|
---
| | | **- FROM THE DESK OF SPEAKER MIKE JOHNSON-**
The real battle has just begun, Peter.

**As your Speaker of the House, I'm officially sounding the alarm: We just missed our April 30th deadline. **

**This comes at the worst possible time because the Radical Left is more determined than ever to undermine our values, destroy our economy, and dismantle our constitutional freedoms.**

**Here's the situation:** Top Democrats like Nancy Pelosi and Hakeem Jefferies have already raised a staggering amount of money to try and take back the House and impose their radical agenda on America. **We need to do everything in our power to stop them.**

That's why I'm turning to you today. We need your help to reach our goal and stay ahead of the Radical Left's money machine.

_This April goal is a one-time opportunity to supercharge our efforts and keep our House majority safe from liberal attacks as we charge into the summer._

name: Deb Haaland
subject: A personal note for you about why I decided to run
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Election Day is just the finish line of this race – it’s what we do now that will determine whether we win or lose.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=1700%2E400544%2EDajDKJ)
I’m emailing to ask you to [**please make your first-ever $5 contribution to our grassroots campaign for New Mexico. Your early support will make a powerful difference.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250502_1_marathon?amount=5&refcode=em20250502-1700&refcode2=1700_400544_DajDKJ&akid=1700%2E400544%2EDajDKJ)
First, I hope you’ll let me share a personal note about this race, and how it reminds me of the other running I’ve been doing: marathons and other road races across the country.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250502_1_marathon?amount=5&refcode=em20250502-1700&refcode2=1700_400544_DajDKJ&akid=1700%2E400544%2EDajDKJ)_When I ran the Boston Marathon in ‘21, my feet pounded the ancestral homelands of the Massachusett, the Mashpee Wampanoag, & the Pawtucket people._
I love running. It’s a way that I honor my ancestors, those who serve our country, and the landscapes that sustain us. It strengthens my spirit, and it’s one of the many things that have made me fierce.
In the 20 years since I started running, I’ve learned many lessons – some more literal than others. Good shoes matter. You fuel for mile seven at mile two. You must treat a marathon or an ultramarathon as what it is: 26 (or more!) miles.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250502_1_marathon?amount=5&refcode=em20250502-1700&refcode2=1700_400544_DajDKJ&akid=1700%2E400544%2EDajDKJ)_I run because my ancestors gave me this ability._
You must be prepared, take things slow, and put in the work well ahead of the race. Runners often say that the training cycle is the marathon, and race day is just the celebration of your preparation – and I believe that!
I know you see where I’m going with this. Running has taught me that winning will require endurance, preparation, and strength. **The work we do together right now will be the defining factor in whether we’re prepared to defeat the MAGA forces already hard at work trying to flip New Mexico red.** It’s why I knew we had to launch our campaign early, and why I’m relying on early support from dedicated supporters like you to build a campaign strong enough to overcome the immense challenges that lie ahead.
**[Winning this race isn’t something I can do without you. Will you donate $5 to power our campaign? Election Day is just the finish line of this race – it’s what we do today that will determine whether we win or lose.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250502_1_marathon?amount=5&refcode=em20250502-1700&refcode2=1700_400544_DajDKJ&akid=1700%2E400544%2EDajDKJ)**
Since my first marathon, I’ve never stopped running. I run races to honor people who serve our country, like the Army Ten-Miler and Marine Corps Marathon. I run to see beautiful landscapes, like the mesas in the 34-mile ultramarathon in Canyon de Chelly National Monument in Arizona.
**Running has taught me what it means to be fierce, and that the things worth fighting for are worth working hard for.** Progress is earned through perseverance. It is realized by facing challenges head-on, whether it’s in a race that carries me many miles on my feet, or a run for Congress, or running an entire department as the United States Secretary of the Interior.
**I’m running for governor to change the game for New Mexico.[ I would be humbled to receive your support in the form of $5 donation, especially early in this race when it will make the biggest impact.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250502_1_marathon?amount=5&refcode=em20250502-1700&refcode2=1700_400544_DajDKJ&akid=1700%2E400544%2EDajDKJ)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250502_1_marathon?amount=5&refcode=em20250502-1700&refcode2=1700_400544_DajDKJ&akid=1700%2E400544%2EDajDKJ)**
With your support, I know we will cross that finish line together 💛
Deb
P.S. This weekend, runners will gather for the Shiprock Marathon — one of the most breathtaking races in the country. It winds through the heart of Navajo Nation, across land that carries stories, prayers, and footprints of generations. Races like this remind me that running is sacred. It grounds us in earth and brings us closer to one another. **As we take on the hard miles ahead in this campaign, I’m holding close the spirit of that race.**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250502-1&t=2&refcode2=1700_400544_DajDKJ&akid=1700%2E400544%2EDajDKJ)**

clean_body: Election Day is just the finish line of this race – it’s what we do now that will determine whether we win or lose. I’m emailing to ask you to First, I hope you’ll let me share a personal note about this race, and how it reminds me of the other running I’ve been doing: marathons and other road races across the country. I ran the Boston Marathon in ‘21, my feet pounded the ancestral homelands of the Massachusett, the Mashpee Wampanoag, & the Pawtucket people. I love running. It’s a way that I honor my ancestors, those who serve our country, and the landscapes that sustain us. It strengthens my spirit, and it’s one of the many things that have made me fierce. In the 20 years since I started running, I’ve learned many lessons – some more literal than others. Good shoes matter. You fuel for mile seven at mile two. You must treat a marathon or an ultramarathon as what it is: 26 (or more!) miles. run because my ancestors gave me this ability. You must be prepared, take things slow, and put in the work well ahead of the race. Runners often say that the training cycle is the marathon, and race day is just the celebration of your preparation – and I believe that! I know you see where I’m going with this. Running has taught me that winning will require endurance, preparation, and strength. The work we do together right now will be the defining factor in whether we’re prepared to defeat the MAGA forces already hard at work trying to flip New Mexico red. It’s why I knew we had to launch our campaign early, and why I’m relying on early support from dedicated supporters like you to build a campaign strong enough to overcome the immense challenges that lie ahead. Since my first marathon, I’ve never stopped running. I run races to honor people who serve our country, like the Army Ten-Miler and Marine Corps Marathon. I run to see beautiful landscapes, like the mesas in the 34-mile ultramarathon in Canyon de Chelly National Monument in Arizona. Running has taught me what it means to be fierce, and that the things worth fighting for are worth working hard for. Progress is earned through perseverance. It is realized by facing challenges head-on, whether it’s in a race that carries me many miles on my feet, or a run for Congress, or running an entire department as the United States Secretary of the Interior. I’m running for governor to change the game for New Mexico. With your support, I know we will cross that finish line together 💛 Deb P.S. This weekend, runners will gather for the Shiprock Marathon — one of the most breathtaking races in the country. It winds through the heart of Navajo Nation, across land that carries stories, prayers, and footprints of generations. Races like this remind me that running is sacred. It grounds us in earth and brings us closer to one another. As we take on the hard miles ahead in this campaign, I’m holding close the spirit of that race.

name: Jeff Merkley
subject: I reintroduced the Equality Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
I am fighting to expand freedom and open the doors of opportunity for everyone.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106974587/543214029/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZjY4NGE0NC04NjI3LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=0mfL6K-ClfBDT3ibIJmQYay3ZP2j_OpiEylY1_-mB8c=&emci=9f551264-7c27-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=ff684a44-8627-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=7731215)
---

| | **This week, I teamed up with my colleagues Senator Tammy Baldwin and Senator Cory Booker to reintroduce the Equality Act, legislation that extends freedom and opportunity to live as their authentic selves to our LGBTQ+ friends and neighbors.** Despite the progress we've made, it's painfully clear: equal justice is still not a reality for everyone in America. The Trump Administration, MAGA Republicans, and right-wing state legislatures are escalating attacks on the rights, security, and freedoms of LGBTQ+ Americans nationwide. Over 850 anti-LGBTQ+ bills have been filed so far across the country this year — the most in U.S. history. **The Equality Act is bold historic legislation to enshrine comprehensive civil rights protections for our LGBTQ+ family members and neighbors in federal law.** This bill is about freedom. The freedom for everyone to fully participate in every aspect of life. Generations of Americans have marched, voted, organized, and raised their voices to fully realize the vision of America as a free and equal nation for all. By leading the Equality Act, I am fighting to expand freedom and open the doors of opportunity for everyone. **Back in 2007, I successfully led the fight to secure this vision for Oregonians, and it is way past time for Congress to do the same for all LGBTQ+ Americans — no exceptions, no compromises — by passing my Equality Act.** Let's get it done! name: All On The Line
subject: [Breaking] FL legislature passes extreme bill on ballot initiatives
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
|
---

Bad news out of Florida today, Peter:
Extremist lawmakers in Florida just voted to make it nearly impossible for citizens to put constitutional amendments on the ballot — directly taking power away from ordinary people.
This bill adds burdensome costs on those seeking to collect signatures — from cumbersome ID requirements to undue fees, unnecessarily short deadlines, and strict regulations geared toward preventing grassroots efforts to reform the state constitution. And if someone makes a mistake, this legislation would subject them to steep fines and even time in prison. Now, it’s on its way to Gov. Ron DeSantis’ desk, where he’s expected to sign it into law.
Let’s be clear: **This bill is designed to silence people’s voices. Anti-democracy attacks are getting more common and more brazen across the country. It’s on people like us to fight back by doing what we do best: Organizing.**
[**We need generous supporters to fund our people-powered work at All On The Line. Rush in a gift today, and let’s make sure that attacks on our democracy like this one don’t go unchallenged.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106974812/543214583/-766669670?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_FLRR_email_20250502-AOTL-Tier1-FLRR_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250502-AOTL-Tier1-FLRR_&utm_campaign=FLRR&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjg4ZDM2MGFlLTg2MjctZjAxMS04YjNkLTYwNDViZGVkOGNjYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=5DyR2ZljoXFtUZ3p2ix-o9Y3hRYUB8sjfGfHmdPh0kM=)

clean_body: | Bad news out of Florida today, Peter: Extremist lawmakers in Florida just voted to make it nearly impossible for citizens to put constitutional amendments on the ballot — directly taking power away from ordinary people. This bill adds burdensome costs on those seeking to collect signatures — from cumbersome ID requirements to undue fees, unnecessarily short deadlines, and strict regulations geared toward preventing grassroots efforts to reform the state constitution. And if someone makes a mistake, this legislation would subject them to steep fines and even time in prison. Now, it’s on its way to Gov. Ron DeSantis’ desk, where he’s expected to sign it into law. Let’s be clear: This bill is designed to silence people’s voices. Anti-democracy attacks are getting more common and more brazen across the country. It’s on people like us to fight back by doing what we do best: Organizing. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thank you. We'll be in touch with more when we know it. The All On The Line Team = is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. In Congress, I knew how important issues like protecting healthcare, lowering prescription prices, and addressing the high cost of living were to Coloradans.
That’s why I took on Big Pharma and special interests, pushed to allow Medicare to negotiate lower prices, and worked to expand the Child Tax Credit to lower taxes for Colorado families.
But since my opponent, Gabe Evans, has taken office, we’ve seen the progress we worked so hard for stripped away. He has ignored Coloradans’ concerns about the impact Trump’s tariffs will have on prices and supported cuts to Medicaid – _just to give a tax break to billionaires._
**Unlike Gabe Evans, I'm running to represent people like you, Peter.[_**That’s why I’m asking you to take my campaign priorities survey and let me know your thoughts as we get further into this campaign. Will you take my survey now?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106974852/543214710/368184596?refcode=email_050225_survey&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYWE5MDgxOS04NzI3LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yn2HQP4yn4ChroD0ci7sl1mw-errH8zDy2bBbBf1fM8=&emci=c7a41ad8-6827-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=2aa90819-8727-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=12666102)**

Hakeem Jeffries is the Democratic Leader of the U.S. House of Representatives. Like many of us, Hakeem was raised by a working-class family; His mom was a social worker and his dad was a substance abuse counselor.

Now, as the top Democrat in the House, he’s fighting to stop MAGA extremism and reclaim the majority for Democrats.

Hakeem is committed to electing a record number of Democrats in the next election and beyond. But he can’t compete with Republicans’ special interest cash without our support. name: With Honor Action
subject: Service 🇺🇸, Ships 🚢, and Schools 🏫
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/80080bc246ac/2-acts-and-a-vp-nominee-14052582?e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | | [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=217081cd58&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | | [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=0aa9478f91&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| Friends,

In case you missed it, Rep. Don Bacon, a founding Co-Chair of the For Country Caucus and Air Force veteran, [wrote an op-ed](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=f426870500&e=33f53a5b1a) in the Washington Post about recent cuts to AmeriCorps that have impeded the agency's ability to field and support community service projects all across the nation.

➡️ In South Carolina, the town of Chester's Senior Companion Program let go [more than a dozen volunteers](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=2819113208&e=33f53a5b1a) immediately.

➡️ In Georgia, AmeriCorps was forced to suspend the work of [67 volunteers](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=4490899ff0&e=33f53a5b1a), effective immediately. They served in health centers, reached out to new patients, and worked at food pantries, schools, and national parks.

We were proud to be with Voices for National Services, the leading national service advocacy organization, for their annual awards dinners on Wednesday evening—lawmakers expressed their commitment to ensuring that service in America remains a cornerstone of our democracy.

To see how you can support service in America, please check out their Action Center [here](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=d4791d3e14&e=33f53a5b1a).
---
|
---
_With Honor Co-Founder & CEO Rye Barcott speaking to over 200 service leaders, lawmakers, and advocates at the 2025 Friends of National Service Awards Dinner._
|
---
| [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=d5fc2de05a&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| _“With Honor Action applauds Senator Mark Kelly, a Navy veteran, and Senator Todd Young, a Marine Corps veteran, for proposing real solutions to revitalize our nation’s shipbuilding base and create more job opportunities for Americans. As advocates for bipartisan, principled veteran leadership in Congress, we are pleased to see veterans who have chosen to continue to serve in Congress working together to address the critical issues facing our nation,” said**Rye Barcott, Co-Founder and CEO, With Honor.**_
---
| In another example of veterans in Congress leading the way, we were pleased to join With Honor Action allies Senators Mark Kelly and Todd Young for the reintroduction of the SHIPS for America Act, comprehensive legislation that has drawn not just bipartisan, bicameral support in Congress but [also has the support of the White House](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=dff344305a&e=33f53a5b1a).
**📣 Watch the press conference ➡️ [YouTube](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=edff628890&e=33f53a5b1a)**

**📄 Read the full announcement ➡️ [Press Release](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=ba1596863b&e=33f53a5b1a)**
---
|
---
|
---
_From left: With Honor Co-Founder & CEO Rye Barcott, With Honor Action ally, Navy veteran, and astronaut, Senator Mark Kelly, and President & CEO of the Center for Naval Analyses Dr. Katherine McGrady._
|
---
| [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=d13ca19f4e&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| Finally, With Honor Action, alongside Student Veterans of America and Princeton University, were thrilled to be cosponsors of Blue Star Family's “Higher Ed to the Hill” event, which spotlighted not only how colleges and universities are making strides to accommodate a new generation of student vets but what insights and experiences student vets are bringing to the table, from their fellow students all the way up to Princeton President Christopher Eisgruber and Fayetteville State University’s Chancellor Darrell Allison.
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | | | Please follow With Honor Action on Twitter via this [link](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=559c0ed5d5&e=33f53a5b1a),
and consider forwarding this to a friend who may be interested in learning -- and doing -- more for our country.

|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106975881/543218316/-896966196?refcode=AU_SMP_FR_GEN_GEN_AC30_20250502_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODY3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDMyYmNhNi04NzI3LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OM9KIdLCR7-Xm6EdljjLH694bLUcXcsHAnIjOAqG_lM=)
**Take a look at these names, team:** These 21 Republican Senators are running for re-election next year. Tommy Tuberville.
Dan Sullivan.
Tom Cotton.
Ashley Moody.
Jim Risch.
Joni Ernst.
Roger Marshall.
Bill Cassidy.
Susan Collins.
Cindy Hyde-Smith.
Steve Daines.
Pete Ricketts.
Thom Tillis.
Jon Husted.
Markwayne Mullin.
Lindsey Graham.
Mike Rounds.
Bill Hagerty.
John Cornyn.
Shelley Moore Capito.
Cynthia Lummis. If Democrats successfully flip four of these seats, then we can take back the Senate majority, hold Donald Trump accountable, and deliver for working families. subject: It’s never been more important that YOUR voice is heard.
body:
Correcting the Narrative on Transgender Radicalism| |
---
| |
---
| |
---
[](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=7e433d5a577144cb96bfa14feea06c84&_e=GbpvEpE2FKITmzPrtsIOnNLIeauzLY7NcCUMbWv_P7purVmbWeuecxok5EehfF5zcPTcyYvsdSNZ0B4J3UFbb620YTyi2MxjOBIl9abG218ELNcYf443gKSvgCFBVbgh7m90ZP5zQkFG8BHwvKSFkdgFSDVnA0rEce6a_NMm1DU55oM6o9fbG7viB_IVYhZnfjS8YLqferA4WHofzFZyDSuEH8sU9maDJleO3VE2JVBtU8akPWRsS-QlNEpXnBvfyRhka48MtNdT6pHBzh36tCINxxDIFuBSB2f4dsG2L2zQHG8teXrvi_Est7-wavB92KK2iQVEWN3QIpCqql3_tfgO3ctNwq8oEmQ0_d23ZR4%3D)
---
| | Dear Fellow Conservative, **[I need you to take AMAC Action’s PARENTS vs. PROPAGANDA: SURVEY ON TRANS RADICALISM right now.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=7e433d5a577144cb96bfa14feea06c84&_e=GbpvEpE2FKITmzPrtsIOnNLIeauzLY7NcCUMbWv_P7purVmbWeuecxok5EehfF5zcPTcyYvsdSNZ0B4J3UFbb620YTyi2MxjOBIl9abG218ELNcYf443gKSvgCFBVbgh7m90ZP5zQkFG8BHwvKSFkdgFSDVnA0rEce6a_NMm1DU55oM6o9fbG7viB_IVYhZnfjS8YLqferA4WHofzFZyDdHaNujdjQItwsh5lSXvns24grY0dm3XO-ErTRbdPQ_jU9COSaMYrBekg7p0kGkjkQyE_d50gtcbYEXq8YWV3Etz2zUEY726b2Mj-ZIAHq77I8ml9R9vEUdzouA5IvJFfin2DNujKPdmBiqBANBJj_A%3D)** Because “pro-trans” organizations are pushing the **false narrative** that the majority of Americans **_are backing the radical transgender movement and_** **_want to block President Trump’s executive orders._** This is more than a political fight—this is a battle for parental rights. The Left wants to take parents out of the equation and hand over life-altering decisions to schools, judges, and radical activists.**But AMAC Action believes parents—not woke ideologues—should have the final say in what’s best for their children.** This battle has never been more important, because a poll from one of these “pro-trans” organizations claims they’ve found **_“High Support Among Parents for Transgender, Nonbinary Children”..._**
---
_“Most adults would be willing to take all actions tested to support their child if they came out as transgender or nonbinary, including: encouraging other family members or friends to respect their gender identity (71%); use their chosen name and pronouns correctly (69%); and not vote for political candidates that support anti-transgender policies (65%)”_
---
Worse, here are some of the supposed “findings” from these radical pro-trans organizations:
---
* “38,600 transgender youth live in states that have “banned access” to facilities, such as bathrooms”.
* “120,200 transgender youth live in states that restrict access to school athletics.”
* 38% of transgender adolescents live in states with laws or policies restricting their access to gender-affirming care.

---
As a senior how do you feel about their findings? _Are you concerned about “38,600 transgender youth being banned from locker-rooms”?__**_Or_**_**...****Are you more concerned about the hundreds of thousands of “transgender” adults _WHO ARE_ right now free to use the restroom with REAL women and girls?** _Are you worried about the 120,200 “transgender youth” living in states that “restrict access to school athletics?”_** __Or_ ..._****Are you scared that your daughters or granddaughters might be forced to compete with,_and be injured by,_ a biological male?** _Are you worried that_ 38% _of transgender adolescents live in states with laws or policies restricting their access to gender-affirming care?_**__Or_ ..._****** **Are you horrified that children are undergoing surgeries that leave them unable to have children of their own, and make them dependent on powerful, lifelong pharmaceutical treatments, sometimes without parental consent or knowledge?****I know what side I stand on. I stand on the side of parental rights and the protection of our children.** **And that’s why I need your immediate response:[Take your AMAC Action PARENTS vs. PROPAGANDA: SURVEY ON TRANS RADICALISM _right now_ to stand with conservative seniors who want to protect America’s children _._](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=7e433d5a577144cb96bfa14feea06c84&_e=GbpvEpE2FKITmzPrtsIOnNLIeauzLY7NcCUMbWv_P7purVmbWeuecxok5EehfF5zcPTcyYvsdSNZ0B4J3UFbb620YTyi2MxjOBIl9abG218ELNcYf443gKSvgCFBVbgh7m90ZP5zQkFG8BHwvKSFkdgFSDVnA0rEce6a_NMm1DU55oM6o9fbG7viB_IVYhZnfjS8YLqferA4WHofzFZyDaPN88ko_n7RyHNcqF_BpQwVasZRqcXcBQoonBvH3E_kHu6gwwk9nYnm63eOZiRkiHn0-n4a3XosFvKn1NfkYhEIQzkABjblxikCqgaECkZrappPRvv_RNQeDpO5cNIaomE2wXPwMWJfdzonxuQSHWw%3D)**
---
[ **TAKE THE SURVEY** ](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=7e433d5a577144cb96bfa14feea06c84&_e=GbpvEpE2FKITmzPrtsIOnNLIeauzLY7NcCUMbWv_P7purVmbWeuecxok5EehfF5zcPTcyYvsdSNZ0B4J3UFbb620YTyi2MxjOBIl9abG218ELNcYf443gKSvgCFBVbgh7m90ZP5zQkFG8BHwvKSFkdgFSDVnA0rEce6a_NMm1DU55oM6o9fbG7viB_IVYhZnfjS8YLqferA4WHofzFZyDTf_gM6j9DEhqfHXep_Cv2F1qCMYCzjCHJzlDaDfnTu19zZKIWL2cYPvw4u7a8asJiOZlYhZuroFOa3gQ1tUF4EjiyKEbAmXGGDCd-MxNCKvplmi46jSeePnQs9JAxpLta4yBpLlzWBLj5kawWG7OZ0%3D)
---
**My fellow patriot, my name is Rebecca Weber and I’m the Chairwoman of AMAC Action. **** ** “AMAC” stands for Association of Mature American Citizens. And when it comes to standing up for the rights of conservative seniors, “action” is what we’re known for. **With its 2 million members, AMAC is the conservative antidote to left-leaning AARP. And our 75 chapters and 300 volunteer delegates make our advocacy group, AMAC Action, a force to be reckoned with on Capitol Hill.** **[And today I’m asking you to stand with us to correct the narrative being pushed by radical organizations.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=7e433d5a577144cb96bfa14feea06c84&_e=GbpvEpE2FKITmzPrtsIOnNLIeauzLY7NcCUMbWv_P7purVmbWeuecxok5EehfF5zcPTcyYvsdSNZ0B4J3UFbb620YTyi2MxjOBIl9abG218ELNcYf443gKSvgCFBVbgh7m90ZP5zQkFG8BHwvKSFkdgFSDVnA0rEce6a_NMm1DU55oM6o9fbG7viB_IVYhZnfjS8YLqferA4WHofzFZyDSj1IHHTlidvNwLF00Dyb8DXllt19Kk4m5U88W87NLPD45il3v01CNZ5iy832sfvCEtFqo4uoG97EWYahy3vpvVYQvc2N-gw61rVOSFKHFGzeecQR8rh12mRE1ek3xDzCWjKmw5z4ZlYoVdZkeqlLJI%3D)** At AMAC Action we know that patriotic Americans like you and I are horrified by stories of school administrators “socially-transitioning” children at school without so much as a phone call to their parents...We’re horrified by the laws being passed by states like Colorado that deem “misgendering” a form of child abuse... And we’re horrified by court rulings like those in California that have barred parents from joint custody because one parent does not want to allow a child to “transition.”It’s happening all around us—but AMAC Action is committed to fighting **_AGAINST_** the madness.But powerful left-wing organizations across the country are fighting **_FOR_** it...The terrifying reality is that **_the radical ACLU alone has over $200 million to spend each year_** fighting for “transgenderism” and other left-wing radicalism in the courts.And they aren’t just _pushing_ the Leftist transgender movement, they are aggressively _forcing_ it on us and our children. From the classroom to social media and seemingly innocuous children’s television shows -- **_transgender ideology is everywhere._** They want to make us believe WE are the radicals for wanting to protect kids.Enough is enough. ****AMAC Action is countering this radical misinformation from the Trans activists with PARENTS vs. PROPAGANDA: SURVEY ON TRANS RADICALISM.**** While President Trump has directed the federal government to recognize only two sexes -- male and female... ...he needs all the help he can get to take on the radical judges and left-wing officials in states like California and Maine who are fighting every single one of his executive orders.During President Trump’s last term, the ACLU bragged about filing over 400 legal actions against the Trump administration.And I fear that this term their sabotage will be much worse. We need definitive proof that the American people are on his side on this critical, common-sense issue.**[I'm counting on you to take AMAC Action’s PARENTS vs. PROPAGANDA: SURVEY ON TRANS RADICALISM.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=7e433d5a577144cb96bfa14feea06c84&_e=GbpvEpE2FKITmzPrtsIOnNLIeauzLY7NcCUMbWv_P7purVmbWeuecxok5EehfF5zcPTcyYvsdSNZ0B4J3UFbb620YTyi2MxjOBIl9abG218ELNcYf443gKSvgCFBVbgh7m90ZP5zQkFG8BHwvKSFkdgFSDVnA0rEce6a_NMm1DU55oM6o9fbG7viB_IVYhZnfjS8YLqferA4WHofzFZyDfV2wGD7g0nXvdNDDbkwTdlddOyDIQE1x2R6Y6Mb7uvfeXVHXGgco6YokQxSsSRAi8WaaMNBu0Hk46c0G6SQT0U8dUsJuns-dM0R_qRm1FaL2k-zt9CPzRD1rdBT946tmCWGt5DJJf1XEm3kjwkxgsw%3D)**
---

clean_body: Correcting the Narrative on Transgender Radicalism| | | | | | | | Dear Fellow Conservative, Because “pro-trans” organizations are pushing the false narrative that the majority of Americans are backing the radical transgender movement and want to block President Trump’s executive orders. This is more than a political fight—this is a battle for parental rights. The Left wants to take parents out of the equation and hand over life-altering decisions to schools, judges, and radical activists.But AMAC Action believes parents—not woke ideologues—should have the final say in what’s best for their children. This battle has never been more important, because a poll from one of these “pro-trans” organizations claims they’ve found “High Support Among Parents for Transgender, Nonbinary Children”... “Most adults would be willing to take all actions tested to support their child if they came out as transgender or nonbinary, including: encouraging other family members or friends to respect their gender identity (71%); use their chosen name and pronouns correctly (69%); and not vote for political candidates that support anti-transgender policies (65%)” Worse, here are some of the supposed “findings” from these radical pro-trans organizations: “38,600 transgender youth live in states that have “banned access” to facilities, such as bathrooms”. “120,200 transgender youth live in states that restrict access to school athletics.” 38% of transgender adolescents live in states with laws or policies restricting their access to gender-affirming care. As a senior how do you feel about their findings? Are you concerned about “38,600 transgender youth being banned from locker-rooms”?Or...**Are you more concerned about the hundreds of thousands of “transgender” adults WHO ARE right now free to use the restroom with REAL women and girls? Are you worried about the 120,200 “transgender youth” living in states that “restrict access to school athletics?” Or ...Are you scared that your daughters or granddaughters might be forced to compete with,and be injured by, a biological male? Are you worried that 38% of transgender adolescents live in states with laws or policies restricting their access to gender-affirming care?_Or ...** Are you horrified that children are undergoing surgeries that leave them unable to have children of their own, and make them dependent on powerful, lifelong pharmaceutical treatments, sometimes without parental consent or knowledge?**I know what side I stand on. I stand on the side of parental rights and the protection of our children. And that’s why I need your immediate response: My fellow patriot, my name is Rebecca Weber and I’m the Chairwoman of AMAC Action. “AMAC” stands for Association of Mature American Citizens. And when it comes to standing up for the rights of conservative seniors, “action” is what we’re known for. With its 2 million members, AMAC is the conservative antidote to left-leaning AARP. And our 75 chapters and 300 volunteer delegates make our advocacy group, AMAC Action, a force to be reckoned with on Capitol Hill. At AMAC Action we know that patriotic Americans like you and I are horrified by stories of school administrators “socially-transitioning” children at school without so much as a phone call to their parents...We’re horrified by the laws being passed by states like Colorado that deem “misgendering” a form of child abuse... And we’re horrified by court rulings like those in California that have barred parents from joint custody because one parent does not want to allow a child to “transition.”It’s happening all around us—but AMAC Action is committed to fighting AGAINST the madness.But powerful left-wing organizations across the country are fighting FOR it...The terrifying reality is that the radical ACLU alone has over $200 million to spend each year fighting for “transgenderism” and other left-wing radicalism in the courts.And they aren’t just pushing the Leftist transgender movement, they are aggressively forcing it on us and our children. From the classroom to social media and seemingly innocuous children’s television shows -- transgender ideology is everywhere. They want to make us believe WE are the radicals for wanting to protect kids.Enough is enough. **AMAC Action is countering this radical misinformation from the Trans activists with PARENTS vs. PROPAGANDA: SURVEY ON TRANS RADICALISM. While President Trump has directed the federal government to recognize only two sexes -- male and female... ...he needs all the help he can get to take on the radical judges and left-wing officials in states like California and Maine who are fighting every single one of his executive orders.During President Trump’s last term, the ACLU bragged about filing over 400 legal actions against the Trump administration.And I fear that this term their sabotage will be much worse. We need definitive proof that the American people are on his side on this critical, common-sense issue. Behind this ideology lies a massive, billion-dollar industry that’s pressuring parents into allowing life-altering surgeries for their children—surgeries that leave kids unable to have children of their own and turn them into lifelong medical patients.We will call this what it is—abuse.We will call them what they are–enablers of abuse and enablers of child mutilation.And we need to know that America’s seniors stand with us in this fight.To secure parental rights, and protect children from radical transgender ideology, you in advance for your help. | | For Freedom, Rebecca Weber,Chairwoman**AMAC Action | | | | | AMAC Action is a 501(c)4 committed to working directly with patriots like you to preserve the values that have made America the greatest nation. The "Action" branch of AMAC, our community-based chapters work tirelessly educating AMAC members and like-minded conservative members of the community and driving legislative results for our country. Your support will make us that much more effective in our fight to defend YOUR values. | | 2032 Tally Rd, Suite 1,Leesburg FL 34748 |

We’ve already invested over $350,000 into Virginia to strengthen candidate recruitment, voter outreach, and more. We have no intention of slowing down – we’re committed to investing seven-figures into Virginia’s legislative races this year.

They’re targeting the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. | [](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=bd933871e180424a9c41541b786bbc81&_e=yKMHm99AQTc4W7ooIqaKffQYkcKthIpO3JDC8c-HMPSqedVcCuNY3Mu6REBZFkDEX_xs71SPfjkNNXS5sjrWYC9fgrQnozkXtaC8B6jvHjH7PQdd9KjcOX0RZxmdgs999HHNz0x0Lm32YVgyqaYVgOHKbVNfmcSIYj-LfgtN9Ooi46uyltD6VmJxHyEveJU3U1BKvKSC4LTagt2J3Mr9wqULEidkfA-LxJVfzlN0mWAbroS3bOx83Oy_Z8Gr1cS_pm3qxp_WN34Kn7-8HAqJ-dOzmWDudr3DaZeSj2TBZF2jrLsCPVsYFLIwfeWB7EdK8rvnQ6XrRmGoc-82Pbj6gvnlGfsW93K849IzuEdw7k51Bh0SASzW74Oj-t3_x7SAtyyGYYwr_T8T4U0Pai2P5w%3D%3D)

[**MESSAGE FROM NAVY SEALS TIM SHEEHY, ELI CRANE, & RYAN ZINKE**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=bd933871e180424a9c41541b786bbc81&_e=yKMHm99AQTc4W7ooIqaKffQYkcKthIpO3JDC8c-HMPSqedVcCuNY3Mu6REBZFkDEX_xs71SPfjkNNXS5sjrWYC9fgrQnozkXtaC8B6jvHjH7PQdd9KjcOX0RZxmdgs999HHNz0x0Lm32YVgyqaYVgOHKbVNfmcSIYj-LfgtN9Ooi46uyltD6VmJxHyEveJU3U1BKvKSC4LTagt2J3Mr9wqULEidkfA-LxJVfzlN0mWAbroS3bOx83Oy_Z8Gr1cS_EK0FbnIRVbkwJF0YZoB2GKBYnArO8Y_uwyYON4mBcNchYReM4aHNVdjnEKN4uzyzUMexVyFdkB9_FlU04ljEDpsiXaOjS4bjH7NKeCRqUwsllakZWtdEgILH8iurQsh9j_rMvKL-LxFF20QEWydxCA%3D%3D)

Peter,

Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one’s shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway.

The out-of-touch fake news “reporters” at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs.

We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can’t abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country.

_**Yet the liberal news corporations, who _desperately_ want to keep the status quo, _attacked us_ for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government.**_

All of us –**Navy SEALs Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke** – are teaming up to stop career politicians and lazy bureaucrats, but we’re up against more than just the DC establishment. We’re up against the liberal media, RINOs, and revenge-seeking Democrats.

**We need you, Peter to keep us on the frontlines, bucking the corrupt status quo! **[](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=bd933871e180424a9c41541b786bbc81&_e=yKMHm99AQTc4W7ooIqaKffQYkcKthIpO3JDC8c-HMPSqedVcCuNY3Mu6REBZFkDEDBnFX4cnZxgaNBioXISth3j0eKIcM8oDrEUb7_eIvD9qodx08HGUB6ehxMENui-RKFsSu6yEDs0mcJHBaNoCuUQVQ3K73CG3nheau9M12JCyx2QUGhz6t9qEvemstgGnC4uQGP45tc2dF08q94wxpMSiZLmRtFerNHfiCHyrrA3otaTMJWkTygdMJpbN9DEcIpuaCDxgpPLXO1EA1i8sl92SdLMRIXgdqdVVO5ZMFWpKW8w1Bi2oxcDZvIim2U6tRq2hpL46wrc3fp-EY4ngRM6lrdbRJq35vKAzzKtwtjyrpN7iofNmIOlMykFzMzI9vlxrqEM7wy1FXTepM_B-yQ%3D%3D)

→ If the media wants to attack us for having a “warrior mentality” – the mentality to do whatever it takes to achieve a mission...

→ If the media wants to attack us for supporting President Trump’s MAGA movement – the movement that says we can make America great...

→ If the media wants to attack us for taking on the government corruption, fraud, and waste – which has been exposed by Trump’s DOGE department...

_**...Then they don’t understand WHY Donald J. Trump and ALL of us were elected!**_

As SEALs, we all shoot straight with each other. We’re going to shoot straight with you, too: **This hit piece was horsesh*t and part of a larger conspiracy to silence us.**

_**If they can silence us, they can sure as hell silence you.**_

**[We’ll stand in their way and hold them back, but we need you to send resources to the frontlines of the MAGA movement right this minute:](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=bd933871e180424a9c41541b786bbc81&_e=yKMHm99AQTc4W7ooIqaKffQYkcKthIpO3JDC8c-HMPSqedVcCuNY3Mu6REBZFkDEX_xs71SPfjkNNXS5sjrWYC9fgrQnozkXtaC8B6jvHjH7PQdd9KjcOX0RZxmdgs999HHNz0x0Lm32YVgyqaYVgOHKbVNfmcSIYj-LfgtN9Ooi46uyltD6VmJxHyEveJU3U1BKvKSC4LTagt2J3Mr9wqULEidkfA-LxJVfzlN0mWAbroS3bOx83Oy_Z8Gr1cS_SahyZcspzOqmeGQ3_GkuMJD2U2I4TFKFBRnXasyQOc_qNvuGAeuinRcW_R0olKA-gCaEbPt29HuJDYImbO4zOV6SM5jxkq6pGxaUPpXrXoqq08qS-ft4cL6XosRNFAgHEjNepZmiyvHJv_f53I8nWA%3D%3D)

_Three Navy SEALs Serving in Congress_ _
__
__
_

|
* * *

"
"I SERVED AS A NAVY SEAL, BUILT ONE OF THE LARGEST AERIAL FIREFIGHTING COMPANIES IN AMERICA CREATING OVER 200 MONTANA JOBS, AND STARTED A VETERAN-LED CATTLE RANCHING AND FEEDING OPERATION. WHETHER IT WAS IN WAR OR BUSINESS, I SEE PROBLEMS AND SOLVE THEM. AMERICA NEEDS CONSERVATIVE LEADERS WHO LOVE OUR COUNTRY. I'M RUNNING FOR THE U.S. SENATE BECAUSE OUR CAMPAIGN IS ABOUT SERVICE, DUTY, AND COUNTRY -- NOT POLITICS AS USUAL."

clean_body: They’re targeting the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. | Peter, Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one’s shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway. The out-of-touch fake news “reporters” at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can’t abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country. Yet the liberal news corporations, who desperately want to keep the status quo, attacked us for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government. All of us –Navy SEALs Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke – are teaming up to stop career politicians and lazy bureaucrats, but we’re up against more than just the DC establishment. We’re up against the liberal media, RINOs, and revenge-seeking Democrats. We need you, Peter to keep us on the frontlines, bucking the corrupt status quo! → If the media wants to attack us for having a “warrior mentality” – the mentality to do whatever it takes to achieve a mission... → If the media wants to attack us for supporting President Trump’s MAGA movement – the movement that says we can make America great... → If the media wants to attack us for taking on the government corruption, fraud, and waste – which has been exposed by Trump’s DOGE department... ...Then they don’t understand WHY Donald J. Trump and ALL of us were elected! As SEALs, we all shoot straight with each other. We’re going to shoot straight with you, too: This hit piece was horsesht and part of a larger conspiracy to silence us. If they can silence us, they can sure as hell silence you. Thank you so much for your time, Tim Sheehy Eli Crane Ryan Zinke Three Navy SEALs Serving in Congress | " "I SERVED AS A NAVY SEAL, BUILT ONE OF THE LARGEST AERIAL FIREFIGHTING COMPANIES IN AMERICA CREATING OVER 200 MONTANA JOBS, AND STARTED A VETERAN-LED CATTLE RANCHING AND FEEDING OPERATION. WHETHER IT WAS IN WAR OR BUSINESS, I SEE PROBLEMS AND SOLVE THEM. AMERICA NEEDS CONSERVATIVE LEADERS WHO LOVE OUR COUNTRY. I'M RUNNING FOR THE U.S. SENATE BECAUSE OUR CAMPAIGN IS ABOUT SERVICE, DUTY, AND COUNTRY -- NOT POLITICS AS USUAL." * | PAID FOR BY TIM SHEEHY FOR MONTANA Use of Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke's military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

| | On Wednesday, I forced a vote in the Senate on my bipartisan bill to roll back Trump's tariffs. It didn't pass, but **several of my Republican colleagues crossed the aisle to support it.** This is further evidence that cracks are starting to form in Donald Trump's disastrous agenda. He is losing support on his trade war. **Presidents should not have the authority to radically reshape our economy without Congress weighing in.** The Senate had a choice this week: allow Trump to continue trampling over our constitutional authority, or step up and retake our power. Unfortunately, Republicans were content to give Trump the green light to keep steamrolling Congress and the American people with his tariffs. Let me be very clear: every single Republican that voted against my legislation is sending the clear message to their constituents – small businesses, workers, farmers, ranchers, you name it – that they support Trump's catastrophic tariffs that are leading our economy into ruin. We learned this week that **in the first quarter of 2025, the economy shrank.** Trump inherited a growing and thriving economy and a booming stock market. In just three months, he's managed to set us back years. Americans are begging for reassurance and to be let off the financial rollercoaster that Trump's tariffs have put us on. And while my bill didn't make it past the finish line, I'm not giving up. I'll keep battling to rein in Trump's worst impulses and deliver much-needed relief to American families. I pledge to keep working to ramp up public pressure on all fronts so we can end this harmful trade war. More soon, _Ron_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Ron Wyden instantly:

| | Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New Pennsylvania Project newsletter!Trump has had one of the busiest 100 days in modern presidential history, [rivaling that of Franklin D. Roosevelt after the Great Depression.](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Ytv4wklg2Nn-2FI0Nk0-2BS-2F8u0-2F5tsl6bda1rPio9A62AGNQLebMlMzebid2LIPWlxD6AXaSWmkU4kAq5R-2B4nLdcQmMXNLOU1owXzuIiZxfLRbGUgn2a7Dx9p-2BqCXRz8i326rS-2F5k6Ih0OhlY7v2qLy-2BQHC9H3YIWL8fCvV3WgpH5JbqKSC_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYDb5aMoURc3DQmAH7VwsJQjkG14Ld1UotmoIXSQfFI1-2F-2BAVvbPR-2F39xJ3DhlWY3FIp-2BPe5RVJ8pZA-2BqEfpZfv4G-2FXo8A4NpgP2VRUsbJO15kKElLLPNMt8a23JaI40NAzl2Mc3plqNU1v1ouug1FZH3E4NBOYvz8NEEpbq1CtfmE0j1-2FxBx7uuKWG4uNJythy) Trump is trying to [expand the power](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Ytv4wklg2Nn-2FI0Nk0-2BS-2F8u3UBBXh23sBGXxJ-2F11OSZxS-2FAYWilkJeELRvA8PbZY5eYyfotAGWmRNQ6wHDTTm3rHqeJ6bboFht1pzEgOieTJNP0-2FCcJEnjNpHWxsRhIQaU-2BNyUT5r6u7lbHWgv-2BVKubQaJyMX5PZU6kDZkeBMoZdIXN-2FWR93ayZk9aHt-2BPgSQuLeIyQn5LctzsaRlACGbyOtZgT8CTFmvkdFszbg9p8xM1BRkV-2BMDskrEz1fpnj2Kui0utDArzgIzrU8nywxSEcE-3Dh0cl_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYDb5aMoURc3DQmAH7VwsJQjkG14Ld1UotmoIXSQfFI1-2F822HdU2nW-2FDB84GmiUytWQhx4M21wGRul6Gb2sRAPL6ffjhXG714QJY3S7nptCLeeIaKduLr3rrwQD4YMCWFRPt6zJNQv5VKqvr7iqW0HGZ6Qf7rkBGTP6taEMnlkNF8KxHcqZMgF5k4orNW08wpO) of the presidency and shift America to the right almost exclusively with executive orders, which can easily be undone and make it harder to shift the blame from policies that[ are unpopular.](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Ytv4wklg2Nn-2FI0Nk0-2BS-2F8u2LvHfcDKOx0a0RQjNBhZr2C06FkIKn2v1CCLnCZExeF9btAJqn4nw7FnVA2v6aqAVWb4JI8rxg92JmL5QGQPQNAiiwWILXnWbjQ19qhevSTBtrmNkjsurS9BdqoOV3st0QUzBNiBAy1z5n4XpzMKZ1u09z_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYDb5aMoURc3DQmAH7VwsJQjkG14Ld1UotmoIXSQfFI1-2F8iBZ-2FM8dihtEI-2BqqniNjGMQQJAExMj1zZAcyl8tpD3Kgmrm35fGCKhV6vPgywku7zXyHGz49ni94ecsit8LX8LKaScTb9k2kJpXtiZqm09pjGA03oqBYWQXQE-2BD6SEKFpPEEF8YgTiYNWpEc6WcoZ) Executive orders may sound authoritative or commanding, but they have a limited reach. Laws are passed by Congress and signed by the president, and courts typically give wide deference to them. They are difficult to overturn. By contrast, executive orders are an order from the president to just the executive branch, and can be undone by the next president with the stroke of a pen. Trump has rescinded executive orders by President Joe Biden and other presidents, going as far back as [a landmark civil rights order from Lyndon B. Johnson](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Ytv4wklg2Nn-2FI0Nk0-2BS-2F8u1MHmeqoq9XF1aNNjbYtXUsemrz4urP5hnvhtN9dO4mW-2BySojHeesQ2KYN8aaNhw8I-2F4G0dIQoOJdAg5A-2FwBTORsreVi4JRrs2a34zkMX6XItV8tyz49t7Jwq9gJ16R8mYp4hkb5QESlHgGhxFt6bjVyC1w_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYDb5aMoURc3DQmAH7VwsJQjkG14Ld1UotmoIXSQfFI19sTW6dbUL5Y3ZFdJN63U3bDoYLSywJX24uGmo9x0OA4KuLq4SbTBQpEyXak4L1aSzb2AZc-2FoGB9x7l68mQlRQLY4Xz7uP96RkdCqTuBGfPfVVUClXtqaMy1Ss1DyGOlnEIew18emJOvYcRfBPwxVbw).Executive orders are a power flex in the social media age. The orders make the president come across like he's doing a lot -- even if that's not really the case. Dozens of Trump’s executive orders have already been blocked in courts. Among the many examples: Judges have temporarily stuck down Trump’s attempt to[ require proof of citizenship to register to **Kadida writes about how state governments can secure our access to the ballot.

"There are simple and effective ways that we could dramatically expand access to the polls. The state Legislature should work to establish an early voting period where voters can go to an early voting location, sign in as quickly and easily as they would at their normal polling place on Election Day, and cast a ballot in a way that is convenient, safe, secure and fits into their busy lives."

"Another aspect of this critically needed legislation, which we have been proud to stand in support of from the beginning, is ending the practice of having an arbitrary cutoff date to register to vote in advance of an election."

"There is a reason the preamble to the U.S. Constitution is centered around the notion of working to 'form a more perfect union.' Democracy is, always has been, and always will be a work in progress for all to fully realize. And in the state where American democracy was born, it’s high time our elected officials put the will of the people first and move forward in this journey towards a more perfect union."

A [preliminary injunction](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9YsTEXwaLouPniNUTHxMrpUqQVtkPYhTn5DPLO85RheFlBUPxIvvcmZw36fJhUmLXGdkq-2B6OHc-2FnW6r1c9gVfALvIZJWwphzfy6Ywu7bc9KurJYg4OLExOY4Ftm02wulq295hr6xq8oBQH2IDZrNf-2BNo-3Dw5A4_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYDb5aMoURc3DQmAH7VwsJQjkG14Ld1UotmoIXSQfFI19-2FFk8I7KVVr5lQpO08s-2FEs-2FauAJXMf-2FEaRjboqpbEu95tsQ5J-2FCOFdCovpZOtMhfy4UR4WcRK1j0lLCUWJsFofpUW5nAelVWlySFiDCOMTjHcJdLLpu7OURLuq9HYHuZLtKd0FZ9v-2FVXjuWbVjhciH) is temporary relief. Courts often look to the public interest underlying the law giving rise to the legal claims at issue in the case to determine whether an injunction would be in keeping with the public interest.The ruling stemmed from a [lawsuit](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Yr0sxQVQzjEF5QK1JkzV825m-2B4LvA5VQ9IX8lMBXGi8eSRL4nNd-2BQIlzMH-2Fy6sURCkLRj6RxIlepGrwObqw7IrFfPpVqpSPo-2BmHXh2TXEbNft5jm8eTvHUETIWHFcCd9p0-2F0MshARu7BDc3sAecf9u6WHdiUEFp484HAlg6q90bPs1Elk0UchDB0jT1pFRpNDCqc7XxwC1BR31Zh91aplyKnm55O8TCQmRjipdpMC5f7clEqQby9-2Bww6HJfzGyprh-2B6XtO4rRJeaGthWPuQq6hJHal1jh89Y6E5DxTRpuPay0Glaql2ZOZvx2vhMHgcEYIpjyJd0sfWIUWEMi-2B3g1Ob7uppXPz4d8iXqP9FhKMldZ84PV88odqDW6xy2mFCZ3T8vPqwSaqzjZE9Pm-2BvGzy9DRVOowsrJ21jYEwqAMQ3ykBPQtVWlDIeP5t4ACotGALbCycSTTWxmZjtbvmGR9P73v-2BSuCcitgNARLgiHmvUQ5iUqdC0XGzemaT6rs2n99-2Fc-2FSfA2l-2FId3Gbu-2FIAUa8hA2f7atxgGsy-2FyL-2F5r8SAYrXMAYIW-2FoE19YDbEmSUMmw-2FPJrtc09brfcGwubWa79ELlmLqGeK-2B91N8Oqj-2Ba0hfncjOOQjE9WgB28xIi2l3JhHNcXY1FSsGeBRUFAmziG537bxwZkFISRcF6wvJwverCBOSaEYUdW-2Bixa8MBt4czB0tQk-2FjLKX4rsfP969Kq7DbpZDVbYEct8dX-2BuTPdyAPZZ-2BExfuDwt2iZVaTcL9-2F1Obi4-2Bq0RSc1zngeC1TE46s-3DsnPD_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYDb5aMoURc3DQmAH7VwsJQjkG14Ld1UotmoIXSQfFI186ZP29l8oB8bOnG-2BoppJzn3bCD-2BS6d4Kv-2B80ZQg9zHIMaci52Cu7-2BuvZ5b5Lbhj6wq8gwSAERFZM1XnKU5zZ-2BC4TGla3Z5vgqrKpM4WW1YLvsXquJOee-2FC-2FMJnUHqEBYN92YR1oRt3N2Otv-2FcYlN2c) brought by the Democratic Party and pro-voting groups challenging Trump’s order as an unprecedented assault on states’ constitutional authority to run their own elections. Trump’s order in general [attempts to make registering](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9YpZUHkN-2BljZaJXzDP365NJpshf9UgaCbaPERSJgV4M1WxhQb1Yd6S4a6BSSzbpE7N1Ehm0zqujhpkApoCk5eQUGSG-2Bgu915OY-2BcCfxy3DCRWW-2FnHomiR31nHb5IuDCfJB9WwLEgGnoHihWL34yqfiOPbNisYNkZQEMDUNcgMSo9A513amR0Ygr5otQZvEvg6r8wDvU-2BD6MkNd4z0Qnxzq-2FpsLtpsApcXwSoHTV6KCRgsq4LTGSn36bqXMujXrDKggnb1c94TpdVx6IfECmj5SvfqAdsG0l4BF9xDgnxrmmFJejX2uv1HZ0mY79pCCbfN2xkhczDsNmpHpveSZooNxY-2F7TzI8Sfh5ojTFu2W-2F6FeBcePj25vM1AvYQj5XsWvRg5FpYB0pi3MiN32O32jA5SLOVH6jc1drBX02r0H4RqEZzWLz2Xmdfjly7kEHGsXcoA-3D-3DeOiq_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYDb5aMoURc3DQmAH7VwsJQjkG14Ld1UotmoIXSQfFI18oBpZJYfUlBnLNQMwAIEh9eH1xDZomNi1oB1yMfNaZ8cMQrmm35leHjqCUvxV1XmMF-2Fx05kvBNPfvoyiY7yZGt3-2BnJazDCX4EwNGzH0-2BYVl5u69kQ0sniatxHUjWA7fgI0tkp5bjF6Hty9YHTZ-2Fw0D), and remaining registered, to vote more difficult. It directs the U.S. Election Assistance Commission (EAC), an agency created by Congress to operate without direct control from the White House, to require eligible voters to provide documentary proof of their citizenship when registering through a federal registration form.District Judge Colleen Kollar-Kotelly suggested that the order vastly exceeded the scope of the president's constitutional powers. “The President is free to state his views about what policies he believes that Congress, the EAC, or other federal agencies should consider or adopt,” the judge wrote. “But in this case, the President has done much more than state his views: He has issued an ‘Order’ directing that an independent commission ‘shall’ act to ‘require’ changes to an important document, the contents of which Congress has tightly regulated.”

“Our Constitution entrusts Congress and the States — not the President — with the authority to regulate federal elections,” the judge added. The judge focused on the [Constitution’s Elections Clause,](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Yq-2B8h3xVKPHApJAs080ky-2BBPtJ9xYFg5JzZ4Ozav4zxfQOTzputavz0mxF3vmVo06vEshj3k7HZXGcv1v76TBy0k26K1dQMunaFY3kidqX5LZFXVOMhem9jOBB-2B24nxgKUanmCEvQ6OsyJRtEuMrVNppPLDkoeRA57V4VmrN-2FuH0cdIk_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYDb5aMoURc3DQmAH7VwsJQjkG14Ld1UotmoIXSQfFI1-2Bp4UoFKO85lXDqlstN95livhJFiQBXvHrX26qnY1uIHKKhNck2RyX3zCZ1wyrN-2FbhGhxAZzVqzIcc7ZrSo97Eo-2Bce3MjTYsj6kgeTLUTdx1Xwgw8zfLDtjXC9WO-2FSgI5jrI9avi2CmDeZ5Vs3COKdm) which gives states the duty to set rules for the “times, places, and manner” of elections — and gives Congress the power to make national voting laws. “I’m glad to see that the provisions of Trump’s unlawful executive order that would have disenfranchised American citizens across the nation have been put to a halt, but the work doesn’t stop here,” Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold said in a statement. “Trump is abusing his power in an attempt to make it harder for voters to fight back at the ballot box. We cannot let him diminish the strength of our elections or dilute the voice of the American people.”In Pennsylvania, this November voters will have the power to retain or vacate three seats of the Pennsylvania Supreme Court.The votes cast by powerful Pennsylvania voters in November will impact our lives for years to come, as Pennsylvania’s Supreme Court will make decisions about our election administration, access to abortion, the safety of our workplaces, and the right to unionize. Make sure you can voice your vote!

# You're registered to vote,
now what?
---
Thursday, May 8, 2025
6:00-7:00PM
via ZOOM
(RSVP for link)

Join us for a powerful series of voter-focused events this May—because democracy works best when we’re all in it, informed and engaged.Whether you’re a first-time voter or a seasoned resident, this event will break down the PA election calendar and show you how to stay civically engaged all year long. Kadida opens with the story of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland father wrongfully deported to El Salvador and detained in one of the world’s most notorious prisons—despite a U.S. Supreme Court ruling calling for his return. She then shares a recent protest experience where someone told her to “go back to Africa,” leading to a larger conversation about the power of protest, Black identity, and diaspora pride. clean_body: | | | | | Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New Pennsylvania Project newsletter!Trump has had one of the busiest 100 days in modern presidential history, Trump is trying to of the presidency and shift America to the right almost exclusively with executive orders, which can easily be undone and make it harder to shift the blame from policies that Executive orders may sound authoritative or commanding, but they have a limited reach. Laws are passed by Congress and signed by the president, and courts typically give wide deference to them. They are difficult to overturn. By contrast, executive orders are an order from the president to just the executive branch, and can be undone by the next president with the stroke of a pen. Trump has rescinded executive orders by President Joe Biden and other presidents, going as far back as orders are a power flex in the social media age. The orders make the president come across like he's doing a lot -- even if that's not really the case. Dozens of Trump’s executive orders have already been blocked in courts. Among the many examples: Judges have temporarily stuck down Trump’s attempt to and , and there’s an ongoing legal battle about whether Trump can proclaim that a Venezuelan gang “,” to justify the use of a wartime law to deport migrants to a foreign prison without due process. | | | | | From Immigrant workers deserve dignity, safety, and respect. WORK IS WORK, no matter who you are or where you’re born. | | | Rally for independent courts held in Carlisle "Rally for independent courts held in Carlisle" “Laws can be repealed. Politicians can be voted out. But court decisions? Those can shape generations,” said Kimeka Campbell, senior director of campaigns and engagement for the New Pennsylvania Project.“If we want a Pennsylvania that is fair, just and free - not just for ourselves, but for those who come after us - we must protect our courts.” Affiliated Organization NPPEF in the News! by our founding CEO Kadida Kenner. Kadida writes about how state governments can secure our access to the ballot. "There are simple and effective ways that we could dramatically expand access to the polls. The state Legislature should work to establish an early voting period where voters can go to an early voting location, sign in as quickly and easily as they would at their normal polling place on Election Day, and cast a ballot in a way that is convenient, safe, secure and fits into their busy lives." "Another aspect of this critically needed legislation, which we have been proud to stand in support of from the beginning, is ending the practice of having an arbitrary cutoff date to register to vote in advance of an election." "There is a reason the preamble to the U.S. Constitution is centered around the notion of working to 'form a more perfect union.' Democracy is, always has been, and always will be a work in progress for all to fully realize. And in the state where American democracy was born, it’s high time our elected officials put the will of the people first and move forward in this journey towards a more perfect union." | On April 24, 2025, the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia published an opinion explaining the court's order granting a preliminary injunction that blocks the Election Assistance Commission from taking steps to carry out key parts of . A is temporary relief. It preserves the status quo until the courts decide on the merits of the case. Courts often look to the public interest underlying the law giving rise to the legal claims at issue in the case to determine whether an injunction would be in keeping with the public interest.The ruling stemmed from a brought by the Democratic Party and pro-voting groups challenging Trump’s order as an unprecedented assault on states’ constitutional authority to run their own elections. Trump’s order in general , and remaining registered, to vote more difficult. It directs the U.S. Election Assistance Commission (EAC), an agency created by Congress to operate without direct control from the White House, to require eligible voters to provide documentary proof of their citizenship when registering through a federal registration form.District Judge Colleen Kollar-Kotelly suggested that the order vastly exceeded the scope of the president's constitutional powers. “The President is free to state his views about what policies he believes that Congress, the EAC, or other federal agencies should consider or adopt,” the judge wrote. “But in this case, the President has done much more than state his views: He has issued an ‘Order’ directing that an independent commission ‘shall’ act to ‘require’ changes to an important document, the contents of which Congress has tightly regulated.” “Our Constitution entrusts Congress and the States — not the President — with the authority to regulate federal elections,” the judge added. The judge focused on the which gives states the duty to set rules for the “times, places, and manner” of elections — and gives Congress the power to make national voting laws. “I’m glad to see that the provisions of Trump’s unlawful executive order that would have disenfranchised American citizens across the nation have been put to a halt, but the work doesn’t stop here,” Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold said in a statement. “Trump is abusing his power in an attempt to make it harder for voters to fight back at the ballot box. We cannot let him diminish the strength of our elections or dilute the voice of the American people.”In Pennsylvania, this November voters will have the power to retain or vacate three seats of the Pennsylvania Supreme Court.The votes cast by powerful Pennsylvania voters in November will impact our lives for years to come, as Pennsylvania’s Supreme Court will make decisions about our election administration, access to abortion, the safety of our workplaces, and the right to unionize. Make sure you can voice your vote! Here is a this year. | | | We Talk Weekly Saturday, May 3, 2025,9:15pmVirtual event WPPM 106.5 FM Check out Kadida Kenner, founding CEO of New Pennsylvania Project during her in-studio interview on We Talk Weekly, Saturday, May 3, 2025, at 9:15pm. Kadida will talk about her continued journey, successes, and upcoming projects and events. Listen to the live interview! You can watch by visiting and clicking "LiveStream." | | | Why Courts Matter Wednesday, May 7, 2025 7:00-8:00PM Virtual event Join from anywhere Join us on Wednesday, May 7th at 7pm for a special virtual presentation on "Why The Courts Matter- Pennsylvania", with Kadida Kenner, founding CEO of New Pennsylvania Project. This incredible advocacy project seeks to protect the independence of our state and federal courts and educate the electorate about their importance. | | | Philadelphia Judicial Candidate Forum Thursday, May 8, 2025 6:00-8:00PM Sam Staten Jr. Training Center 1333 North Broad St Philadelphia, PA 19122 Philadelphia Community Stakeholders, convened by Ryan Boyer, is hosting a judicial candidate forum.Get to know the Philadelphia judicial candidates who are asking for your vote this Primary Election Day. | | Check out these upcoming events from the affiliated organization New PA Project Education Fund! | | | Solidarity for Immigrants Monday, May 5th, 2025 11AM State Capitol Steps Third St. and State St, Harrisburg, PA 17120 Join the affiliated organization New PA Project Education Fund, along with over 300 people representing 18 community organizations from across Pennsylvania who will gather at the State Capitol to rally for immigrant rights. | | | You're registered to vote, now what? Thursday, May 8, 2025 6:00-7:00PM via ZOOM (RSVP for link) Join us for a powerful series of voter-focused events this May—because democracy works best when we’re all in it, informed and engaged.Whether you’re a first-time voter or a seasoned resident, this event will break down the PA election calendar and show you how to stay civically engaged all year long. Learn your power. Use your voice. | | | Pittsburgh School Board Candidate Forum Friday, May 9, 2025 6:00-7:30PM Hosanna House 807 Wallace Ave Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools. | | | Allegheny Judicial Candidate Forum Monday, May 12, 2025 6:00-8:00PM Hosanna House 807 Wallace Ave Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before they appear on your ballot. Your vote shapes our justice system! | | | We have a new podcast! And the second episode launched Thursday, April 24! And check out episode three next Thursday afternoon! — where the misunderstood voice becomes the culture’s power is hosted by our Founding CEO, Kadida Kenner, and Charles Gregory , from In this deeply personal and politically charged episode of Slingshot The Podcast, host Kadida Kenner delivers truth with urgency and unapologetic pride.Titled “We Don’t Have to Stay Here,” Episode 2 explores systemic injustice, personal protest, and what it means to be rooted—and displaced—at the same time. Kadida opens with the story of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland father wrongfully deported to El Salvador and detained in one of the world’s most notorious prisons—despite a U.S. Supreme Court ruling calling for his return. She then shares a recent protest experience where someone told her to “go back to Africa,” leading to a larger conversation about the power of protest, Black identity, and diaspora pride. Listen to us on , , , , and . Plus, watch us on Connect with us: Instagram: @slingshotthepodcast Facebook: @SlingshotThePodcast Youtube: @SlingshotThePodcast TikTok: @slingshotpodcast Email: info@newpaproject.org SlingshotThePodcast.org | | | | | The affiliated organization New PA Project Education Fund is on the ground registering voters and talking about the upcoming primary election! Philadelphia County | | | | | Dauphin County | | | | | Harrisburg Luzerne County | | | | | | | Ballots have dropped! Ballots in the following counties have been mailed out to voters so far: Allegheny, Lehigh, Luzerne, Lackawanna, Northampton, Delaware, and Montgomery. Spotlight PA's is live with information you can use as you prepare to vote this year! Get answers to your questions with our Everything you need to , | | | Guides to ; Guides to , Guides to Guía para Action YOU Can Take: Yesterday, reported that Justice Department leaders “have removed all of the senior civil servants working as managers in the department’s voting section and directed attorneys to dismiss all active cases, part of a broader attack on the department’s civil rights division.” The in large measure to protect the voting rights of Black formerly enslaved people after the Civil War, and the Civil Rights Division is considered a crown jewel. Now the cop has been pulled off the beat. These efforts are reflected in the SAVE Act, which if enacted would be the worst voting law ever passed by Congress. It would require citizens to produce a passport or birth certificate to register or even to re-registerIf the SAVE Act is defeated — if, in fact, it never comes to a vote — let’s not just move on. This would be a signal victory. History doesn’t repeat, but it rhymes. The fight to vote has been a defining struggle throughout American history. Always, people have had to press to get or keep access to our democracy, and just as consistently, some have tried to shrink the circle.It is now headed to the Senate. Senator Fetterman and McCormick have both sent responses to constituents on their current stance on the SAVE act bill. The responses can be found below. Fetterman’s letter: McCormick’s letter: This bill is a form of voter suppression. If it becomes law, this bill will disenfranchise millions of eligible voters, especially immigrants, Black and brown communities, married people, and young people. Democracy works when everyone has access to the ballot. Let’s protect that. Calling your U.S. Senators only takes a few minutes, but can have huge impact! You can do your part by calling our senators and asking them to protect our voting rights by voting NO on this bill, You can even use this script | | | | | | | | | | Thank you for staying informed and engaged! | | Our Contact Information New Pennsylvania Project P.O. Box 443 West Chester, PA 19381 4126996771 | | | | | ---|---|---

name: Sarah McBride
subject: One of the most painful memories of my life
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Long Run PAC, P
body:
No matter how much they attack me, I’m not backing down.
#
Hi Friend, it’s Congresswoman Sarah McBride.
Recently, I sat on the Capitol steps and shared one of the most painful memories of my life: holding my husband Andy’s hand as he took his last breaths after a battle with cancer.
That experience is why I will never stop fighting Republican attempts to cut access to health care.
**Predictably, Stephen Miller jumped at the opportunity to distract his audience by using schoolyard taunts against me.**
[](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=longrunpac-em-mcbride.dTFiSG&refcode=em250502-1-90&t=1&akid=5540%2E196590%2EWJDyVJ)
